title: Add google-services.json for Firebase/Google sign-in wiring
description: Gradle and manifest wiring for Google Services is in place, but :app:assembleDebug fails because google-services.json is missing from the app module. Add the Firebase config file for the appropriate build variant and re-run the build to verify the plugin processes it.

title: 5.2 Add Compose screens for the study schedule, item details, and offline/error states
description: Add the screens for the schedule view, item details, and offline/error messaging. These should sit on top of the local cache and sync state behavior from the preceding tasks.

title: 5.3 Add unit tests for mapping, repository behavior, and sync conflict handling
description: Add tests that cover event-to-study mapping, repository cache behavior, and sync conflict handling. The test plan should exercise the actual production paths for the sync architecture change.

title: 5.1 Build the Compose flow for connecting an account and showing sync state
description: Create the Compose UI that connects a Google account and presents sync state to the user. This should reflect the sign-in and sync lifecycle defined in the spec and design.

title: 5.4 Run the Android build and test suite for the sync integration
description: Run the Android build and test suite to verify the calendar-sync-firestore-architecture integration compiles and the new behavior is covered. This is the verification step for the full change.

title: 4.3 Implement Firestore write-through for user edits to study metadata
description: Persist study metadata edits to Firestore so cloud-backed data stays consistent across devices. This should reflect the separate study data storage requirement and the Firestore decision in the design.

title: 4.4 Implement deferred retry handling for offline changes and sync failures
description: Add the outbox and retry path for offline changes, failed syncs, and recovery after connectivity returns. This directly supports the offline cache and deferred sync requirement.

title: 4.2 Implement one-way import from Google Calendar into the local projection
description: Import Calendar events into the local projection so the schedule can be rebuilt from Google Calendar as the source of truth for time. Follow the calendar-backed study events requirement and the import behavior described in the design.

title: 3.1 Define Room entities and DAOs for calendar projections, study records, and pending sync operations
description: Define the local persistence model for calendar projections, study records, and the sync outbox. Source requirements come from the tasks file, design doc, and the offline cache requirement in the spec.

title: 3.2 Implement repositories that prefer the local cache before the network
description: Build repositories that read from Room first and only hit the network when necessary. Use the local cache and deferred sync decisions in the design as the guiding behavior.

title: 2.3 Add sign-out and session restore behavior
description: Implement sign-out plus session restore for the current user so the app can recover the signed-in Google/Firebase session on relaunch. This closes the auth loop defined in the spec and design.

title: 4.1 Create Firestore document models for study metadata, progress, and sync timestamps
description: Model the Firestore documents that hold app-specific study data outside Calendar fields, including metadata, progress, and sync timestamps. This should match the cloud storage decisions in the design and the separate study data storage requirement in the spec.

title: 3.3 Add mapping fields between Google Calendar event IDs and study record IDs
description: Add the stable event-to-study mapping fields needed to avoid duplicate records when the same calendar event is synced again. Align with the explicit mapping decision in the design and the stable mapping requirement in the spec.

title: 1.3 Add local test fixtures or sample config for development
description: Add any local development fixtures, sample config, or test scaffolding needed by the authentication and sync work. Use the OpenSpec task list and design doc as the source of requirements.

title: 2.1 Implement Google sign-in entry points with Credential Manager
description: Implement the user-facing sign-in entry points using Credential Manager. This should satisfy the Google account connection requirement in the spec and support the auth flow defined in the design.

title: 1.1 Add Android app dependencies for Firebase, Google auth, Calendar API, Room, and coroutines
description: Add the Gradle dependencies required by the calendar-sync-firestore-architecture change. Source of truth: /home/marcello/projects/4contrib/MyStudy/openspec/changes/calendar-sync-firestore-architecture/tasks.md and /home/marcello/projects/4contrib/MyStudy/openspec/changes/calendar-sync-firestore-architecture/design.md. Include Firebase, Credential Manager, Google ID, Calendar API, Room, and coroutine libraries.

title: 2.2 Exchange Google identity for a Firebase Auth session
description: Convert the signed-in Google identity into a Firebase Auth session so the app has a stable user ID for cloud-backed study data. Align with the identity decision in the design and the Google account connection requirement in the spec.

title: 1.2 Wire manifest and Gradle config for Google sign-in and Firebase
description: Add the Android manifest entries and build wiring required for Google sign-in and Firebase configuration. Reference /home/marcello/projects/4contrib/MyStudy/openspec/changes/calendar-sync-firestore-architecture/tasks.md, /home/marcello/projects/4contrib/MyStudy/openspec/changes/calendar-sync-firestore-architecture/design.md, and /home/marcello/projects/4contrib/MyStudy/openspec/changes/calendar-sync-firestore-architecture/specs/study-calendar-sync/spec.md.

title: Scaffold Google Calendar sync architecture
description: Prepare the Android app for Google-based calendar sync. Add the initial data model and app structure for sign-in, calendar sync, and hidden account-scoped storage so implementation can proceed without committing to a backend yet.
